//: EMULATOR-ONLY config (firestore + auth + FUNCTIONS). Kept SEPARATE from firebase.json on purpose: the deploy workflow (.github/workflows/deploy_19.yml) runs a bare `firebase deploy`, so a firestore/functions block in firebase.json would push to PRODUCTION (fir-sample-aae4a) — forbidden. Boot with: e2e/scripts/deploy-cf-emulator.sh  (which sets functions/package.json main -> index.emulator.js for the run, materializes functions/.secret.local, then runs `firebase emulators:start --project starlabs-cicd --config firebase.emulator.json`). The Firestore emulator partitions data by project id, so the seed, the Angular app, and the Flutter app must all use the SAME id (starlabs-cicd). functions.source resolves the `starlabs-cloud-function` symlink in the hub.
//databases: The Firestore emulator serves the DEFAULT db AND any named db (e.g. firestore-forms) on demand — named DBs are created on first access, no per-db block needed here. The named `firestore-atc` DB is intentionally never touched (index.emulator.js excludes the ATC functions). rules/indexes below apply to the default db.
//functions: source points at the nested CF repo (branch test/queue-e2e-deploy). NO `predeploy` loopDetector here — loopDetector.js runs against functions/index.js which `require`s the ATC module (getFirestore('firestore-atc') at module-eval); the emulator must load functions/index.emulator.js instead (the deploy script repoints package.json main). codebase id matches the cloud deploy for parity.
//firestore: rules/indexes apply to the DEFAULT db. The Firestore EMULATOR (firebase-tools) does NOT support multiple databases / per-named-db rules yet, so the `firestore-forms` named db cannot be given permissive rules here — its CLIENT reads/writes default to DENY (Admin-SDK seeds still reach it, rules-bypassed). profiles PA-13/14 (client read/write of formsByClient) therefore test.skip on the emulator and run against the cloud test project instead. Do NOT switch this to the multi-db array form: the emulator rejects it ("does not support multiple databases yet") and then finds NO rules file at all.
